---
title: "Rust API"
---

The Rust API reference is generated by `rustdoc`, separately from the Python
API pages generated by `quartodoc`.

Build the complete docs site with:

```bash
make docs-build
```

The rendered website includes the Rust reference here:

[Open the Rust API reference](rustdoc/reducers/index.html)

For Rust-only iteration, run:

```bash
make docs-rust
```

That writes the raw rustdoc output to `target/doc/reducers/index.html`.

Keeping item-level Rust API docs in `rustdoc` is the standard Rust workflow.
`make docs-build` copies that rustdoc tree into the Quarto output so the
published site contains both the Python and Rust references.

Core modules:

| module | purpose |
|---|---|
| `reducers_1d` | Whole-slice reducers and fused helpers such as `minmax`. |
| `axis` | Normalized 2-D axis reducers used by the Python layer. |
| `finite` | `Float` and `ScanPolicy`, shared by scan kernels. |
| `parallel` | Runtime thread and grain controls. |
